codeReview.summary: The shared Marcel helper is well-factored and its isolated unit tests pass, but two promised behaviors remain unintegrated in real feature generation: first-year player fallback is unreachable in production, and roster-turnover weighting is never supplied by any production caller.

issues.description: Lineup/player blends still use team prior baselines instead of the required league-average fallback for rookies. `compute_offensive_features()` always calls `_apply_metric_blend()` with `prior_value=team_priors.get(...)` (see also `src/features/pitching.py:180-186` for starters), and repo-wide usage shows `is_first_year=True` is only exercised in `tests/test_marcel_blend.py:56`. The first-year-player behavior exists in the helper API but is not reachable in production feature computation.

The main feature-generation path invokes `compute_offensive_features()`, `compute_pitching_features()`, and `compute_defense_features()` without `roster_turnover_by_team` (`src/model/data_builder.py:649-670`), and repo-wide search found no other production caller supplying that argument. As shipped, the >40% roster-turnover prior-weight reduction never occurs outside the isolated helper unit test, so this contract requirement is not actually implemented end-to-end.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission guidance still documents a simplified Marcel pattern that no longer matches the intended contract. AGENTS shows only a single `regression_weight=30` formula and omits the per-metric 30/15/50 defaults, league-average rookie fallback, and roster-turnover adjustment.

`engine-worker` says it handles Marcel blend integration, but its procedure and verification checklist are still tailored to edge/Kelly/settlement work and do not tell workers how to implement or validate Marcel-specific behavior. That mismatch likely contributed to the helper-only coverage for rookie/turnover handling.

summary: Review failed. The commit adds a solid centralized Marcel helper and refactors offense/pitching/defense callers, but the required first-year-player and roster-turnover behaviors are only implemented/tested at the helper level and are not wired into production feature generation.
